abstract.t: This document extends the Tempo Payment Method [draft-tempo-payment-method] with support for escrowed payment locks. It defines the lock intent for incremental payments suitable for streaming and metered services, where clients deposit funds into an on-chain escrow that permits the payee to withdraw funds up to the amount authorized by signed releases, while permitting the payer to withdraw any remaining funds after expiry.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: The base Tempo Payment Method [draft-tempo-payment-method] supports one-time charges, authorizations, and subscriptions. However, these intents require the payment amount to be known upfront or bounded by a fixed authorization.

For metered services—where consumption is only known as the service is delivered—a different model is required: one where clients deposit funds into escrow, receive service, and authorize incremental releases as they consume.
This extension defines the lock intent, which establishes an escrowed payment lock using:

ul.li: TIP20Escrow interface for on-chain fund escrow (implementable as a

section.t: smart contract or chain-native precompile)

ul.li: Off-chain EIP-712 releases for incremental release authorization
Payee-controlled settlement for efficient fund collection

section.name: Example Use Case: Metered Agent Execution
section.t: This section is non-normative.
Consider a user delegating tasks to an AI agent that consumes API tokens:

ol.li: User initiates an agent task (e.g., a research task)
Agent's API returns 402 with a lock challenge
User creates a lock with a budget for the task
As tokens are consumed, the client sends EIP-712 releases authorizing payment
Server settles periodically or when the task completes

section.t: The user pays for tokens consumed, with unused budget reclaimable after lock expiry. This enables users to delegate open-ended tasks to agents with bounded spending.

section.name: Trust Model and Fund Commitment
section.t: In this document, the server acts as the payee, and the client acts on behalf of the payer.
Payment locks use an escrow-based model with on-chain fund locking:

ol.li: Funds are locked at creation. The payer deposits TIP-20 tokens into

section.t: a TIP20Escrow implementation. These funds are locked until release or expiry.

ol.li: Payee can release at any time. Using a signed release from the payer,

section.t: the payee can withdraw funds from the escrow (up to the released amount).

ol.li: Partial release is supported. The payee can release multiple times,

section.t: each time withdrawing the delta between the release amount and previously released amount.

ol.li: Payer can withdraw after expiry. If the payee fails to release before

section.t: the lock expires, the payer can withdraw all remaining (unreleased) funds.

ol.li: Payee bears the risk of not settling. The only risk to the payee is

section.t: failing to release before expiry; escrowed funds are committed for payment.
Trust properties:

ul.li: Payer protection: Funds can only be released by payee with a valid

section.t: signed release; after expiry, payer reclaims unreleased funds.

ul.li: Payee protection: Escrowed funds are committed for payment for valid

section.t: releases (no risk of payer draining wallet).
section.artwork:
Lock Lifecycle:
CREATE SERVICE RELEASE EXPIRY │ │ │ │ ▼ ▼ ▼ ▼ ┌─────────┐ ┌───────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ │ Deposit │ │ Releases │ │ Payee │ │ Payer │ │ to │─────▶│ signed │────▶│ withdraws│────▶│ withdraws│ │ escrow │ │ (off-chain)│ │ delta │ │ remainder│ └─────────┘ └───────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ │ │ └──────────────────────────────────┘ Funds locked in escrow (guaranteed for payee)
section.t: Cumulative release semantics:
Releases MUST authorize a cumulative total, not incremental deltas. A release's cumulativeAmount MUST be strictly greater than the previously accepted cumulativeAmount for the same lock.

ul.li: Release #1: cumulativeAmount = 100000 (authorizes 100000 total)
Release #2: cumulativeAmount = 250000 (authorizes 250000 total)
Release #3: cumulativeAmount = 400000 (authorizes 400000 total)

section.t: When settling, the implementation computes: delta = cumulativeAmount - released
This permits the lock to remain usable after partial releases—the payer continues signing releases with higher cumulative amounts up to the deposit.

section.name: Relationship to Base Specification
section.t: This document extends [draft-tempo-payment-method] with:

ul.li: A new lock intent (Section 4)
Lock-specific request and credential schemas (Sections 5-6)
EIP-712 release format (Section 7)
TIP20Escrow interface (Section 8)
Lock-specific verification and settlement procedures (Sections 9-10)

section.t: All other aspects of the Tempo Payment Method apply unchanged.

section.name: Requirements Language
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

dd.t: An on-chain escrow where the payer deposits TIP-20 tokens that can only be released to a designated payee using signed releases, or withdrawn by the payer after expiry.
An EIP-712 signed message authorizing a cumulative release amount for a specific lock. Releases are monotonically increasing in amount.
An interface for payment lock escrow, providing lock, release, requestRelease, and withdraw operations. Implementations MAY be deployed as smart contracts or chain-native precompiles.
A unique identifier (bytes32) for a specific lock, returned by a TIP20Escrow implementation when a lock is created.
The total amount authorized for release since lock creation. Each release's cumulativeAmount MUST be greater than the previous.

section.name: Intent: "lock"
section.t: An escrowed payment lock for streaming or metered services. The payer deposits funds into an on-chain escrow (a TIP20Escrow implementation) that permits the payee to withdraw funds up to the amount authorized by releases, while permitting the payer to withdraw any remaining funds after expiry.
A lock request MUST include the parameters defined as REQUIRED in Section 5. A server challenge for intent="lock" MUST NOT omit any REQUIRED fields.

section.name: Lock Request Schema
section.t: For intent="lock", the request parameter contains a base64url-encoded JSON object with the following fields:

tr.td: Amount to lock in base units (stringified number)
Lock expiry as UNIX timestamp in seconds (stringified number)
Exclusive intermediary authorized to call release (if set, payee cannot)
If true, server will pay transaction fees (default: false)

section.t: If trust is present, only trust can call release(); the payee cannot. Funds are still transferred to payee, but trust acts as an exclusive intermediary (e.g., an arbitration service). If trust is absent, payee calls release() directly.
The following is a non-normative example:
In this example, the request specifies a lock of 10.00 alphaUSD (10000000 base units) expiring at UNIX timestamp 1737043200.
The client MUST fulfill this by signing a Tempo Transaction that:

ol.li: Calls lock(asset, amount, expiry, payee, trust) on a TIP20Escrow

section.t: implementation

ol.li: The implementation transfers amount of asset from the payer to escrow
Returns a lockId that identifies this specific lock

section.t: The trust field, if present, specifies an additional address that can release funds (e.g., an arbitration service). If omitted, only the payee can release funds before expiry.

section.name: Lock Credential
section.t: For intent="lock", the credential includes a lockId field that identifies the created lock on-chain. The client MUST broadcast the lock creation transaction and obtain the resulting lockId before sending the credential.
The payload object for lock credentials:

tr.td: Transaction hash of the lock creation transaction
Lock identifier returned by the TIP20Escrow implementation

section.t: Example:

section.name: Release Credential
section.t: Release credentials are transmitted out of band (for example, via HTTP POST to an endpoint defined by the server). The credential includes the EIP-712 message and signature:

tr.td: Original challenge ID from the lock session

section.t: The payload object for release credentials:
The message object:

tr.td: Total amount authorized for release (cumulative, base units)
UNIX timestamp after which release expires

section.t: Example:

section.name: EIP-712 Release Format
section.t: To release funds from a lock, the payee MUST provide an EIP-712 signed release from the lock owner. The signed release authorizes the payee to withdraw funds up to cumulativeAmount from the lock.

section.name: Domain Separator
section.t: The EIP-712 domain MUST use the following values:

tr.td: Address of the TIP20Escrow implementation that created the lock

section.name: Release Type

tr.td: Total amount authorized for release (cumulative, not delta)
UNIX timestamp after which this release expires

section.name: Cumulative Semantics
section.t: Releases MUST authorize a cumulative total, not incremental deltas. A TIP20Escrow implementation MUST reject any release where cumulativeAmount <= released. A payee SHOULD use the highest valid release available when calling release().

ul.li: Release #1: cumulativeAmount = 100000 → releases 100000
Release #2: cumulativeAmount = 250000 → releases 150000 more (delta)
Release #3: cumulativeAmount = 400000 → releases 150000 more (delta)

section.t: The implementation computes: delta = cumulativeAmount - lock.released
This prevents replay attacks and permits the lock to remain usable after partial releases—the payer continues signing releases with higher cumulative amounts up to the locked deposit.

section.name: TIP20Escrow Interface
section.t: The TIP20Escrow interface defines the escrow functionality for payment locks. Implementations MAY be deployed as:

ul.li: Smart contracts: Standard EVM contracts deployable on any EVM chain
Precompiles: Chain-native implementations at fixed addresses for

section.t: improved gas efficiency
The interface is described using Solidity for exposition; non-Solidity implementations MUST provide equivalent semantics.

section.name: Lock State
section.t: Each lock is identified by a unique lockId and stores:

tr.td: UNIX timestamp after which payer can withdraw
If set, exclusive caller for release (payee cannot call)

section.name: lock
section.t: Creates a new payment lock with escrowed funds.
The implementation MUST:

ol.li: Transfer amount of asset from msg.sender to the implementation
Compute expires = block.timestamp + duration
Generate unique lockId
Store lock state with released = 0
Emit LockCreated event
Return lockId

section.name: release
section.t: Payee releases funds using a signed release.
The implementation MUST:

ol.li: If lock.trust is set: verify msg.sender == lock.trust

section.t: If lock.trust is not set: verify msg.sender == lock.payee

ol.li: Verify block.timestamp < lock.expires
Verify EIP-712 signature from account (lock owner)
Verify cumulativeAmount > lock.released (monotonicity)
Verify cumulativeAmount <= lock.amount (cap)
Compute delta = cumulativeAmount - lock.released
Transfer delta to lock.payee
Update lock.released = cumulativeAmount
Emit FundsReleased event

section.t: The implementation MUST reject releases after expiry.

section.name: requestRelease
section.t: Payer requests early release of the lock.
The implementation MUST:

ol.li: Verify msg.sender is lock owner
Set lock.releaseRequested = block.timestamp
Emit ReleaseRequested event

section.t: After the implementation-defined grace period, the payer can call withdraw(). The grace period SHOULD be documented by the implementation.

section.name: withdraw
section.t: Payer withdraws remaining funds after expiry or grace period.
The implementation MUST:

ol.li: Verify msg.sender is lock owner
Verify either:

section.t: - block.timestamp >= lock.expires, OR - lock.releaseRequested > 0 && block.timestamp >= lock.releaseRequested + gracePeriod

ol.li: Compute remaining = lock.amount - lock.released
Transfer remaining to lock owner
Mark lock as inactive
Emit LockClosed event

section.name: modifyLock
section.t: Payer adds funds and/or extends expiry on an existing lock.
The implementation MUST:

ol.li: Verify msg.sender is lock owner
Verify lock is active
If additionalAmount > 0:

section.t: - Transfer additionalAmount of the lock's asset from payer to escrow - Add to lock.amount

ol.li: If newExpiry > lock.expires:

section.t: - Update lock.expires = newExpiry

ol.li: Emit LockModified event

section.t: The implementation MUST reject if newExpiry < lock.expires (expiry can only be extended, not shortened).

section.name: getLock
section.t: Query lock state.

section.name: Lock Verification
section.t: For intent="lock" credentials, servers MUST verify the lock state on-chain before granting access:

ol.li: Extract lockId from the credential payload
Call getLock(payer, lockId) on the TIP20Escrow implementation
Verify the returned lock state:

section.t: - active is true - asset matches the requested token - amount >= the requested minimum amount - expires MUST be greater than the current time. Servers SHOULD enforce an implementation-defined minimum remaining lifetime suitable for the requested service. - payee matches the server's address

ol.li: If trust was specified in the request, verify it matches

section.t: Servers SHOULD cache lock state and periodically re-verify for long-running services.

section.name: Release Verification
section.t: Servers receiving a type="release" credential MUST perform the following checks:

ol.li: Verify id matches an active lock session
Extract release fields: lockId, cumulativeAmount, expires, signature
Verify cumulativeAmount > previously received release amount (monotonicity)
Verify expires > current time
Verify EIP-712 signature matches the lock owner (see Section 7)
Store the release for later settlement

section.t: Servers SHOULD acknowledge receipt of valid releases. Servers MAY batch multiple releases and settle periodically rather than per-release.

section.name: Settlement Procedure
section.t: For intent="lock", settlement requires a signed release from the lock owner. The signed release authorizes the payee to withdraw funds up to cumulativeAmount from the lock, enabling incremental authorization for streaming services.

section.name: Settlement Flow
section.t: The following is an illustrative (non-normative) flow:

ol.li: Client signs and broadcasts a transaction with lock() call
Lock is created; client receives lockId
Client sends lock credential via Authorization: Payment header
Server verifies lock state on-chain and begins service
As service streams, client signs EIP-712 releases
Client sends release credentials via Authorization: Payment header
Server collects releases; settles periodically
Server calls release(payer, lockId, amount, signature) with latest release
Implementation verifies signature and transfers delta to payee

section.name: Post-Expiry Recovery
section.t: After expiry, the lock owner can reclaim all unreleased funds by calling withdraw(lockId) (no signature required—only the lock owner can withdraw their own funds after expiry).

section.name: Early Release
section.t: If the payer wants to end the lock before expiry:

ol.li: Payer calls requestRelease(lockId) on the TIP20Escrow implementation
The implementation-defined grace period begins
Payee can still call release() with any pending releases during grace
After grace period, payer can call withdraw(lockId) to reclaim remaining funds

section.t: This mechanism permits the payee to settle outstanding authorized amounts during the grace period while permitting the payer to recover unspent funds thereafter.

section.name: Lock Security
section.t: Implementations MUST consider the following security properties:
Expiry Management: Servers MUST release funds before the lock expires. After expiry, the payer can reclaim all remaining funds. Servers SHOULD:

ul.li: Set alerts for approaching lock expiry
Release funds well before expiry (with margin for transaction confirmation)
Track all active locks and their expiry timestamps

section.t: Payee Address Verification: Clients MUST verify the payee address in the lock request matches the expected server. A malicious server could specify a different address to redirect funds.
Lock Amount: Clients SHOULD limit locked amounts to those consistent with their risk tolerance. While expiry protects against indefinite fund locking, funds remain unavailable until expiry.

Trust Address: If the trust field is specified, only that address can call release(); the payee cannot. Clients SHOULD only accept trusted arbitration services and verify the trust address is legitimate.
Partial Release: TIP20Escrow implementations support partial releases. Servers SHOULD release only the amount consumed to minimize client exposure. Clients can reclaim unreleased funds after expiry.
Early Release (requestRelease): Payers can request early release before expiry. This starts an implementation-defined grace period during which:

ul.li: The payee can still call release() with valid releases
After the grace period, the payer can call withdraw() to reclaim funds
Servers SHOULD monitor for ReleaseRequested events and settle promptly

section.t: This permits payer flexibility while providing the payee time to finalize settlement.

section.name: Release Security
section.t: Implementations MUST consider the following EIP-712 release security properties:
Replay Prevention: Releases use cumulative semantics—each release authorizes a total amount, not a delta. The implementation enforces:

ul.li: cumulativeAmount > lock.released (monotonicity)
cumulativeAmount <= lock.amount (cap)

section.t: This prevents replay: submitting the same release twice has no effect after the first settlement.

Release Expiry: The expires field limits release validity. Clients SHOULD use short windows during active streaming to limit exposure if a release is intercepted. Implementations MUST reject releases where block.timestamp > expires.
Signature Verification: Implementations MUST:

ul.li: Use strict ECDSA recovery to derive signer address
Verify signer matches the lock owner (account parameter)
Prevent signature malleability attacks
Bind signature to lockId, cumulativeAmount, and expires

section.t: Release Interception: Even if an attacker intercepts a release:

ul.li: They cannot submit it (only payee/trust can call release())
They cannot modify the amount (signature verification fails)
They cannot use it on another lock (bound to lockId)

section.t: Off-chain Release Delivery: Clients send releases to servers out of band (for example, via HTTP POST to a server-defined endpoint). The release credential includes both the EIP-712 message and signature.
When release credentials are transmitted over HTTP, the client MUST use TLS . Servers SHOULD acknowledge receipt of valid releases.

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: This document registers the following payment intent:
Intent: lock Method: tempo Reference: This document

section.name: Challenge
section.sourcecode: HTTP/1.1 402 Payment Required WWW-Authenticate: Payment id="lock_kM9xPqWvT2nJrHsY4aDfEb", realm="video.example.com", method="tempo", intent="lock", request="eyJhc3NldCI6IjB4MjBjMDAwMDAwMDAwMDAwMDAwMDAwMDAwMDAwMDAwMDAwMDAwMDAwMSIsImFtb3VudCI6IjEwMDAwMDAwIiwiZXhwaXJlcyI6IjM2MDAiLCJwYXllZSI6IjB4NzQyZDM1Q2M2NjM0QzA1MzI5MjVhM2I4NDRCYzllNzU5NWY4ZkUwMCJ9"
section.t: Decoded request:

section.name: Release Request
section.sourcecode: POST /payments/release HTTP/1.1 Host: video.example.com Authorization: Payment eyJpZCI6ImxvY2tfa005eFBxV3ZUMm5KckhzWTRhRGZFYiIsInBheWxvYWQiOnsidHlwZSI6InJlbGVhc2UiLCJtZXNzYWdlIjp7ImxvY2tJZCI6IjB4NmQwZjRmZGYuLi4iLCJjdW11bGF0aXZlQW1vdW50IjoiNTAwMDAwIiwiZXhwaXJlcyI6IjE3MzcwNDMyMDAifSwic2lnbmF0dXJlIjoiMHhhYmMxMjMuLi4ifX0
section.t: Decoded credential:

section.name: Acknowledgements
section.t: The authors thank the Tempo community for their feedback on payment lock design.
